skill.name: amazon-buy-box-monitor-api-skill
skill.description: Extract Amazon Buy Box, pricing, seller, fulfillment, and availability data for a supplied ASIN through the BrowserAct API.
skill.summary: Monitor Amazon Buy Box and seller data by ASIN using BrowserAct.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found network access, environment variable access, shell invocation guidance, and a critical combined-behavior heuristic. Review confirmed these patterns support the documented BrowserAct API workflow, with no prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration found. The remaining risk is medium because the skill handles an API key and sends ASIN and marketplace data to a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Credential Use
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a Bearer token to the BrowserAct API. This is required for the stated function, but it exposes a sensitive credential to a third-party service and needs clear user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs an Authorization header from BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it in requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior is intentional for the API client, so this is a handling risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: External Data Submission to BrowserAct
medium_findings.description: The script submits the user-provided ASIN and marketplace URL to BrowserAct, then polls for task results. This is expected for the skill, but users should understand that product identifiers and marketplace choices leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The payload and polling endpoints are visible and match the stated BrowserAct workflow. There is no evidence of hidden endpoints or unrelated data collection.

medium_findings.title: API Key Sharing Guidance
medium_findings.description: The user guidance says to provide the API key to the agent or set it as an environment variable. Asking users to provide secrets in chat increases accidental disclosure risk, even though the script also supports environment variables.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly handles a missing API key and the script tells users they may provide it to the agent. This is a real but manageable secret-handling concern.

low_findings.title: Documented Script Invocation
low_findings.description: The markdown includes a command that runs the bundled Python script with ASIN and marketplace arguments. This is normal for a script-based skill, and the example quotes the arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is static documentation for invoking the local script. I did not find evidence of dynamic shell construction or command substitution.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts appear to be caused by product text, ASIN references, status messages, and API response checks. No cryptographic operation or host reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, filesystem probing, process listing, or host inspection. The flagged lines are descriptive text and API control flow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent Over Network
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses the BrowserAct API key in an Authorization header for outbound requests. This is expected, but it is still a sensitive network pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header and requests calls are explicit. The destination is the documented BrowserAct API endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Secret Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment. This is safer than hardcoding, but marketplace users should be warned that the skill uses a secret.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The os.getenv call and missing-key handling are direct evidence. No evidence found that the key is printed after being read.

content.user_title: Monitor Amazon Buy Box Data by ASIN
content.value_statement: Amazon sellers need current Buy Box and competitor pricing data for specific listings. This skill uses BrowserAct to return structured ASIN, seller, price, fulfillment, and availability details.
actual_capabilities: Runs a bundled Python script that starts a BrowserAct workflow for one ASIN.
Accepts an Amazon marketplace URL to target a regional marketplace.
Polls BrowserAct task status until completion or a five minute timeout.
Returns product title, Buy Box owner, price, currency, and availability when provided by the API.
Includes other seller pricing, shipping fee, rating, and feedback data when available.
Detects missing or invalid BrowserAct API key errors and reports them to the user.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY before it can run.
Depends on BrowserAct and Amazon page availability, so results may fail or change.
Processes one ASIN per script invocation.
Does not store history or create long-term price trend reports by itself.

use_cases.title: Check Buy Box Ownership
use_cases.description: Find the current Buy Box owner, price, availability, and fulfillment method for a specific ASIN.

use_cases.title: Compare Competitor Sellers
use_cases.description: Review other seller prices, shipping fees, ratings, and feedback counts for a product listing.

use_cases.title: Validate Regional Listings
use_cases.description: Run the same ASIN against a selected Amazon marketplace URL to inspect regional listing details.

prompt_templates.title: Check One ASIN
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Amazon Buy Box monitor for ASIN B005O2ZU68 on https://amazon.com/ and summarize the current Buy Box result.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Other Sellers
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Amazon Buy Box monitor for this ASIN and list other sellers by total price, rating, and fulfillment method.

prompt_templates.title: Review Regional Marketplace
prompt_templates.prompt: Check ASIN B005O2ZU68 on https://amazon.co.uk/ and report the Buy Box owner, currency, availability, and seller details.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Failed Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the monitor for this ASIN. If it fails, inspect the terminal output, retry once when appropriate, and explain the final error.

output_examples.output: A concise report with the product title, Buy Box owner, current price, currency, stock status, fulfillment method, and seller rating.

output_examples.input: Compare sellers for this ASIN on amazon.co.uk.
output_examples.output: A ranked seller summary showing Buy Box status, seller names, item prices, shipping fees, ratings, and feedback counts.

output_examples.input: The monitor returned an authorization error.
output_examples.output: A clear explanation that the BrowserAct API key is missing, invalid, or expired, with instructions to update the environment variable.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable instead of pasting it into chat.
Use a complete Amazon marketplace URL that matches the region you want to inspect.
Verify important price decisions against Amazon directly before making business changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for bulk scraping without confirming BrowserAct and Amazon policy requirements.
Do not paste private API keys into prompts, tickets, or shared transcripts.
Do not treat one lookup as a historical trend or long-term monitoring record.

faq.question: What API key does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs BROWSERACT_API_KEY for the BrowserAct API. Store it as an environment variable when possible.

faq.question: Does it scrape Amazon directly from my machine?
faq.answer: No. The local script sends the request to BrowserAct, which runs the configured workflow.

faq.question: Can I choose a marketplace region?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a marketplace URL such as https://amazon.com/ or https://amazon.co.uk/.

faq.question: What data can it return?
faq.answer: It can return title, Buy Box owner, price, currency, fulfillment method, availability, other sellers, ratings, and feedback counts.

faq.question: What happens if the BrowserAct task fails?
faq.answer: The guidance recommends one retry unless the output shows an invalid authorization error.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses network access and a sensitive API key.
